blocks.content: Although permission was granted more easily than expected, Shirone had anticipated this situation from the start.
The haves live freely. It was the same when the Ogent Family risked their lives to move the Great Library.
The butlers meticulously categorized the books according to the rules, but the nobles didn't even care whether the books were lost or not.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Eliza hugged Shirone and kept repeating something, but lost in thought, none of her words remained in him.
Suddenly, Eliza seemed to remember something and abruptly stood up.

blocks.speaker: Eliza
blocks.content: This isn't the time for this. I can't have my first meal with my son dressed like this. Dear, I'm going to change my clothes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do as you wish.
Leaving Shirone behind, Eliza walked to the dressing room, followed by a line of female attendants.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone didn't think clothing should matter that much when it came to having a meal. But just as there's a dress code at noble parties, it seemed that choosing the right attire was just as important to royalty as finding her son after 18 years.
With Eliza gone, only Orkamph and Shirone were left. An awkward silence hung between them.
Whether Orkamph had intended it from the start or simply couldn't bear the awkwardness any longer, he spoke for the first time.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Shirone… was it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, it's the name my parents gave me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone emphasized that it was a name given by his parents. Orkamph might have had a name for him too, but Shirone didn't want to abandon the name he had been given.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I see. It's a good name.
Contrary to his expectations, Orkamph didn't say much.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It occurred to him that perhaps there had never been a name given to him in the first place. If that were true, it meant his parents had decided to abandon him even while he was still in his mother's womb.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do you resent me?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I did when I was young. But now…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone swallowed the words he was about to say. It felt too rude to say that he was actually glad to have been born as Vincent's son.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I suppose you don't anymore.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's….

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I understand. Royalty may seem glamorous, but it's not all that great. There are many responsibilities, and much that must be sacrificed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Much like how you sacrificed me.
Orkamph was momentarily taken aback by Shirone's sharp remark, but soon returned to his calm expression.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I'm sorry. It wasn't that I didn't want to find you…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone shook his head, cutting off Orkamph's words. He didn't want to hear it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine. You've found me now, haven't you?

Orkamph felt Sirone's extraordinary nature. Despite living as a commoner for 18 years, there was not a trace of unease even in the foreign royal palace. Furthermore, he possessed the mental fortitude to assert his demands even before the king and queen.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: 'I see…. Blood is truly thicker than water, after all?'
Having reached this conclusion, Orkamph turned toward his study to proceed to the next step.
Then, realizing that Shirone would be left alone, he turned back and asked, 'Dinner is in 30 minutes. Would you prefer to stay here?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I'll step out. Is it alright if I take a look around the palace?
This request was driven by pure curiosity, without any ulterior motive.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Of course. You may explore anywhere you wish. After all, this will soon be your home.

Shirone had every intention of returning to the magic academy. However, he felt it wasn't the right time to bring that up, so he simply expressed his gratitude for Orkamph's hospitality and left the room.
Walking down the corridor with a sense of excitement, Shirone wondered where to start.
The armory, bakery, and chapel were all appealing options, but what intrigued him the most was the gallery, where various artworks were displayed.
As he turned a corner in the corridor, he saw an elderly official standing there.
Shirone approached him to ask for directions, but before he could say anything, the official bowed first.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Sir Shirone, there is someone who wishes to meet you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt his mood sour as soon as he heard the official's words.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Later. I want to be alone right now. His Majesty said I could go anywhere I want until dinner.
He didn't know the rank of the official, but Shirone openly showed his displeasure.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Of course, that's fine as well, but wouldn't it be better for you to meet this person?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sensing an unsettling tone in the official's smile, Shirone suppressed his irritation and regained his composure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And who is this person?

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: It's His Highness Terraje Zion, the current First Prince of Kazra. At least, for now.
The official placed deliberate emphasis on 'for now.' It implied that the position of the First Prince might soon change hands, but for some reason, the tone seemed to suggest the opposite.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hearing the name Zion made Shirone more cautious. The group he had to be most wary of in the royal palace was undoubtedly Terraje's faction. And now, a key figure from that faction had initiated contact.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright, I'll meet him.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: A wise decision, sir. Please, this way.
The official led Shirone toward the eastern wing of the inner quarters.
The place where the royal family resided was smaller in scale than the part where Orkamph stayed, but it was still sufficiently luxurious.

In the hall that marked the division of the different wings stood a towering lion statue that reached the ceiling, and from there, four paths stretched out in the directions of east, west, south, and north.
After walking about 20 meters to the east, Shirone was met with a door adorned in gold.
When the official opened it from the outside, an artificial garden with a floor of white marble awaited.
Before entering, Shirone took a moment to observe the interior.
The garden was beautiful, but it was also the perfect place for someone to hatch a scheme. Despite it being winter, palm trees evoking a tropical feel were planted throughout.
At a table in the center, a younger pair of siblings were enjoying tea, while a sleek black cat and a fluffy white cat played around on the floor.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: Sir Zion, I have brought Sir Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Let him in.
With the command given, the official flashed a sly smile and gestured for Shirone to proceed.
Raising his chin with determination, Shirone walked forward with a straight posture.
The sound of the mysterious official closing the door behind him felt strangely eerie.
As Shirone approached the table, he disregarded manners and scrutinized the faces of the siblings with an intense gaze.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, these are...
Terraje Zion and Terraje Uorin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They were the current First Prince and Princess of Kazura.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, they were Orkamph's children. However, their surname was that of their mother, Terraje. The reason was simple: in the power game of political marriage, Orkamph had lost.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was widely known as Orkamph's humiliation.
Zion finally turned his head to look up at Shirone. Then, as if finding Shirone's serious expression amusing, he joked lightheartedly.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Haha! Don't be so tense. I'm not going to eat you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone didn't know what the Empress Terraje of Kashan looked like, but by stripping away the features of Orkamph from Zion's face, he could vaguely deduce the Empress's appearance.
Zion was a beautiful young man with silver hair that had a cold tone, deep double eyelids, and the elegant curve of a sharp, cat-like chin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This alone made it easy to imagine that Terraje's beauty must be beyond extraordinary.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hello, I'm Uorin. Broter, you're that magician, right? I saw you in that magazine.
The white cat jumped onto Uorin's lap.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone couldn't take his eyes off the fourteen-year-old girl, who held the cat and gently stroked its fur.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If Zion's appearance gave Shirone a sense of what Terraje might look like, Uorin's presence made him feel as if Terraje herself were standing before him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She resembled Terraje far more than Zion did. No, to be more precise, it was as if there was hardly any trace of Orkamph's blood in her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She was a beautiful young girl, possessing an appearance that went beyond beauty and exuded an almost spiritual, mysterious aura.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone had experienced this sensation once before—when he first encountered Ikael in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He never imagined he would feel that same awe toward a human.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: W- Why are you looking at me like that? We'll be family in just a few days, you know. That kind of stare makes me uncomfortable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I didn't mean it that way….

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hohoho! Just kidding, just kidding! I heard you're 18, but you're so innocent. Come on, sit over here.
Shirone composed himself and took the seat Uorin offered.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ei, what does it matter? I'll just call you brother then. You're older than me anyway. By the way, brother, can you use magic?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Magic? Uh, just the basics.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Wow! That's awesome! You have to show me some time! Magic is always so fascinating. How can fire come out of your hands like that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was increasingly engulfed by an odd feeling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There wasn't a hint of any strategic undertone in Uorin's behavior. He even began to wonder if she was simply a kind and cheerful girl.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But a mountain village girl she was not; as the daughter of the Empress, Terraje, she had to be sharper than most people.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then, Shirone suddenly realized what Zion and Uorin had in common.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They weren't afraid of anything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Zion's overt hostility and Uorin's overly friendly demeanor were only possible because they had the immense power of Terraje backing them.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You're not an idiot, so you must be aware of the state of affairs in the royal palace. You also understand the weight of the name Terraje. I called you here to settle things cleanly. I don't know what father is thinking, but you'll never be king anyway. So, it'd be wise not to walk around with your head held too high.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't worry. I only came because I was invited. Once this is all over, I'll go back. I have no intention of living here, and I certainly have no desire to become the king.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hahaha! You speak well. People who say things like that usually end up stabbing you in the back, don't they? Just like the typical deceitful mage. Oh, wait! You're not even a real magician yet, are you? Just an aspiring one.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Brother, don't be so harsh. Shirone had a tough childhood. It's amazing that he managed to enter the magic academy as a commoner. And now, he's considered one of the most promising talents.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Oh, wait! You're not even a real magician yet, are you? Just an aspiring one.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Brother, don't be so harsh. Shirone had a tough childhood. It's amazing that he managed to enter the magic academy as a commoner. And now, he's considered one of the most promising talents.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ah, is it okay if I call you brother?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, nothing's set in stone yet. But I suppose it's fine?
